message: Improve progress feedback and add e2e test

 {"id":"xf-15","title":"Error Messages: Helpful & Actionable","description":"## Purpose

Transform error messages from correct-but-cold to helpful-and-actionable. Every error should guide the user toward resolution.

## Current Problems

1. **Errors are technically correct but unhelpful**:
   Current: 'Error: Cannot use --replies-only and --no-replies together.'
   Missing: WHY they conflict and WHAT to do instead.

2. **No suggestions for common mistakes**:
   When database doesn't exist, we say so but don't explain the full workflow.

3. **No 'did you mean?' suggestions**:
   Typos in commands/types get generic 'unknown' errors.

4. **Error prefix is plain**:
   Just 'Error' in red. Could be more visually distinct.

5. **Missing context**:
   Some errors don't explain what the user tried to do.

## Solution

### Error Format Standard
```
✗ [Brief error title]

   [Explanation of what went wrong and why]

   [Actionable suggestions:]
     • First option
     • Second option

   [Optional: Link to docs or help command]
```

### Specific Improvements

**Conflicting flags**:
```
✗ Conflicting options

   --replies-only and --no-replies cannot be used together.
   These flags are mutually exclusive.

   Choose one:
     --replies-only    Show only replies
     --no-replies      Exclude replies from results
```

**Missing database**:
```
✗ No archive indexed yet

   Before searching, you need to index your X data archive.

   Quick start:
     1. Download your data from x.com/settings/download_your_data
     2. Run: xf index ~/Downloads/twitter-archive

   Need help? Run: xf help index
```

**Unknown type**:
```
✗ Unknown type: 'twet'

   Did you mean 'tweet'?

   Valid types: tweet, like, dm, grok
```

### Implementation Notes

- Use Levenshtein distance for 'did you mean?' suggestions
- Create a helper function for consistent error formatting
- Errors should work well with and without colors

## Files to Modify

- src/main.rs: All anyhow::bail! calls
- src/repl.rs: Error handling in execute()
- src/cli.rs: Possibly custom error types
- src/error.rs: Add formatting helpers

## Dependencies

Part of Visual Design System for consistent error styling.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T09:48:04.345461-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-01-11T10:43:44.838916-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T10:43:44.838916-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented structured error formatting with did-you-mean suggestions. Error messages now include visual distinction (✗), clear explanations, and actionable hints.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"xf-15","depends_on_id":"xf-12","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"},{"issue_id":"xf-15","depends_on_id":"xf-13","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"}]}
 {"id":"xf-16","title":"CLI Help: Examples & Clarity","description":"## Purpose

Improve CLI help text to be more useful, with examples and better organization. Help should enable users to accomplish tasks without trial and error.

## Current Problems

1. **--types lists unsearchable types**:
   The search --types flag shows: tweet, like, dm, grok, follower, following, block, mute, all
   But follower, following, block, mute are NOT searchable (no text content). Including them causes confusion.

2. **No examples in help**:
   Help shows flags but not how to use them together. Users must guess.

3. **Global options repeated everywhere**:
   --db and --index appear in every subcommand help, cluttering the output.

4. **Option descriptions are minimal**:
   '--since <SINCE>  Show only tweets from this date onwards'
   Doesn't mention supported formats or examples.

5. **No common use cases**:
   Users often want to do specific things (search my replies, find DMs with person X).
   Help doesn't show these workflows.

## Solution

### Fix --types values
Remove non-searchable types from the search command:
- Keep: tweet, like, dm, grok
- Remove: follower, following, block, mute
- Decide: keep or remove 'all' (it works but includes non-searchable types)

### Add examples section to search help
```
Examples:
  xf search "hello world"              # Basic full-text search
  xf search "rust" --types tweet       # Search only tweets
  xf search "meeting" --types dm       # Search DMs
  xf search "2024" --since "last week" # Recent content
  xf search "bug" --limit 50           # More results
```

### Improve option descriptions
```
--since <DATE>
    Filter to content from this date onward.
    
    Formats: 2024-01-15, "last week", "3 days ago", "yesterday"
    Example: --since "last month"
```

### Add common workflows to main help
```
Common tasks:
  Search tweets:     xf search "query"
  Search DMs:        xf search "query" --types dm
  View tweet thread: xf tweet <id>
  Export data:       xf export tweets --format csv
  Check health:      xf doctor
```

## Files to Modify

- src/cli.rs: All clap attribute help text, possible_values
- Possibly add custom help templates

## Implementation Notes

Clap supports:
- #[arg(value_hint = ValueHint::...)] for shell completion
- #[arg(long_help = "...")] for detailed help (-h vs --help)
- #[command(after_help = "...")] for examples section","notes":"Completed: search --types now restricted to searchable content types via SearchType; search help includes examples + date formats; top-level help includes Common tasks; added unit help test and e2e help_text_test.sh with detailed logging.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T09:48:25.531146-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-01-11T12:49:12.841898874-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T12:49:01.097365193-05:00","close_reason":"Completed: searchable types only, help examples, date format guidance, tests + e2e script","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"xf-16","depends_on_id":"xf-12","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"}]}
 {"id":"xf-17","title":"REPL Experience: Interactive Polish","description":"## Purpose

Polish the interactive REPL shell to feel modern and intuitive. The REPL is where power users spend their time, so it should be delightful.

## Current Problems

1. **Startup message is plain**:
   'xf interactive mode. Type 'help' for commands, 'quit' to exit.'
   Doesn't show what archive is loaded, document counts, or version.

2. **Prompt doesn't explain context**:
   'xf [42]>' - what does 42 mean? First-time users won't understand.
   Should be 'xf (42 results)>' or similar.

3. **'Goodbye!' is casual**:
   Premium apps say 'Session ended.' or just exit silently.
   'Goodbye!' feels unprofessional.

4. **No inline hints**:
   When prompt is empty, could show ghost text like 'search |type a query'

5. **No version displayed**:
   Users don't know what version they're running.

6. **No recent searches**:
   Could show last few queries for quick re-execution.

7. **Help could be more visual**:
   Current help is dense text. Could use boxes/tables.

## Solution

### Enhanced Startup Banner
```
╭──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│  xf shell v0.5.0                                             │
│                                                              │
│  Archive: @username                                          │
│  Tweets: 12,345 • DMs: 2,341 • Likes: 8,234                  │
│                                                              │
│  Type 'help' for commands, 'quit' to exit                    │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

### Improved Prompts
- Normal: 'xf> '
- With results: 'xf (42 results)> '
- In conversation: 'xf [DM: abc123...]> '

### Professional Exit
Replace 'Goodbye!' with either:
- 'Session ended.' (formal)
- No message, just exit (minimal)
- 'Done. Searched 1,234 queries this session.' (informative)

### Inline Hints (Optional)
Use rustyline's hint feature to show ghost text suggestions.

### Help Improvements
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ COMMANDS                                                    │
├──────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ search <q>   │ Search all content (alias: s)               │
│ list <type>  │ List tweets, likes, dms, etc (alias: l)     │
│ show <n>     │ Show full details of result #n              │
│ more         │ Show next page of results (alias: m)        │
│ export <fmt> │ Export as json or csv (alias: e)            │
│ quit         │ Exit the shell (alias: q)                   │
└──────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Files to Modify

- src/repl.rs: Startup banner, prompts, print_help(), exit message
- Cargo.toml: May need version const

## Dependencies

Should follow Visual Design System guidelines for consistent styling.","notes":"Add terminal-width-aware banner/prompt formatting with clean fallback when width is narrow or stats missing. Test plan: unit tests for prompt/banner formatting helpers, plus an e2e script that runs REPL with scripted input (help, search, quit) and logs command, stdout/stderr, exit code, timing, and env (including NO_COLOR=1).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T09:48:49.02782-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-01-11T10:53:35.208100449-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T10:53:22.826183-05:00","close_reason":"Enhanced REPL startup banner with version, archive info, and document counts. Changed exit message to professional 'Session ended.' styling.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"xf-17","depends_on_id":"xf-12","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"},{"issue_id":"xf-17","depends_on_id":"xf-13","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"}]}
-{"id":"xf-18","title":"Progress & Feedback: Responsive UX","description":"## Purpose

Provide responsive feedback during operations so users never wonder 'is it working?'. Good progress UX builds trust and makes waits feel shorter.

## Current Problems

1. **Progress bar style is dated**:
   Uses '##-' progress chars which looks like 1990s software.
   Modern tools use smooth gradients or spinners.

2. **No ETA during indexing**:
   Long indexing operations don't show estimated time remaining.

3. **No item counts during progress**:
   During indexing, shows '5/8' for data types but not item counts.
   Would be better: 'Tweets: 12,345 indexed'

4. **No timing shown after completion**:
   'Indexing complete!' doesn't say how long it took.

5. **No timing on search**:
   Users don't see 'Found 42 results in 3ms', missing the speed selling point.

6. **No feedback for quick operations**:
   Very fast operations complete silently. Could show brief success.

## Solution

### Modern Progress Style
Replace '##-' with Unicode block characters:
'█▓▒░' or smooth gradient '████████░░░░░░░░'

Consider: spinner for indeterminate, bar for determinate.

### Itemized Progress
```
Indexing @username's archive...

  ✓ Tweets         12,345 indexed (2.1s)
  ✓ Likes           8,234 indexed (1.4s)
  ◐ DMs             processing... 1,234/2,341
  ○ Grok            pending
  ○ Followers       pending
  ○ Following       pending

  Elapsed: 00:05 • ETA: ~00:03
```

### Completion Summary
```
✓ Indexing complete in 8.3 seconds

  Content indexed:
    Tweets:       12,345
    Likes:         8,234
    DMs:           2,341
    Grok:            456
    
  Total: 23,376 documents
  
  Run 'xf search <query>' to start searching.
```

### Search Timing
'Found 42 results in 3ms'

For slow queries (>100ms): 'Found 42 results in 234ms (try narrowing your search)'

### Quick Operation Feedback
For sub-second operations: '✓ Done (12ms)'

## Files to Modify

- src/main.rs: cmd_index(), cmd_search(), all command handlers
- Possibly add a progress module for consistent handling

## Implementation Notes

- Use indicatif crate for progress (already a dependency)
- Consider ProgressStyle::default_spinner() for indeterminate ops
- Use Instant::now() + elapsed() for timing","notes":"Include progress output that degrades cleanly in non-tty; add timing summaries for index/search. Test plan: unit tests for duration formatting and progress summary helpers; e2e script that runs indexing/search on a small fixture archive and logs command, stdout/stderr, exit code, timing, and env (including NO_COLOR=1).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T09:49:11.910125-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-01-11T10:53:45.750858183-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"xf-18","depends_on_id":"xf-12","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"}]}

 {"id":"xf-19","title":"Data Formatting: Human-Friendly Display","description":"## Purpose

Format data for human consumption, not machine parsing. Numbers, dates, and identifiers should be presented in the most useful form for each context.

## Current Problems

1. **Large numbers not formatted**:
   Shows '12345678' instead of '12,345,678'.
   Hard to parse at a glance.

2. **Dates are inconsistent**:
   - Sometimes: '2024-01-15 10:30'
   - Sometimes: '2024-01-15 10:30:00 UTC'
   - Never relative: '2 hours ago'
   ISO format is machine-friendly but not human-friendly.

3. **Tweet IDs shown in full**:
   '1234567890123456789' takes space and provides no value.
   Users rarely need to see the full ID.

4. **CSV export missing \r handling**:
   The run_export function replaces \n but leaves \r intact.
   Could cause issues in some spreadsheet software.

5. **Byte sizes not formatted consistently**:
   Some places show '1234567 bytes', others show '1.2 MB'.

## Solution

### Number Formatting
Add thousands separators: '12,345,678'

Possibly: abbreviate very large numbers: '12.3M' (configurable)

Create helper: format_number(n: i64) -> String

### Date Formatting
Smart relative format based on age:
- < 1 minute: 'just now'
- < 1 hour: '23 minutes ago'
- < 24 hours: '3 hours ago'
- < 7 days: '3 days ago'
- < 1 year: 'Jan 15'
- >= 1 year: 'Jan 15, 2023'

Create helper: format_relative_date(dt: DateTime<Utc>) -> String

For precise contexts (export, JSON), use ISO format.

### ID Display
- Hide IDs by default in text output
- Show on demand or in 'show' command
- Truncate if needed: '1234...6789'
- Always full in JSON/CSV output

### CSV Export
Fix \r handling: .replace('\r', ' ') alongside .replace('\n', ' ')

### Byte Size Formatting
Standardize on: 1.2 KB, 3.4 MB, 5.6 GB
Already have format_bytes() in search.rs - reuse it.

## Files to Modify

- Create src/format.rs (or add to existing module) for helpers
- src/main.rs: Use helpers throughout
- src/repl.rs: Use helpers for display
- src/stats_analytics.rs: Number formatting

## Implementation Notes

Consider using humantime crate for relative dates if not already a dependency.

The chrono crate's format! options can handle most date formatting.

Locale-aware formatting (1,234 vs 1.234) is complex - start with US format.","notes":"Progress: relative date helper + deterministic tests added for search output; CSV escaping now strips CR/LF in search + REPL export with unit test. Remaining: centralize formatting helpers, apply across other outputs, and implement number/ID/byte formatting tasks.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T09:49:33.057934-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-01-11T10:44:29.859347-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T10:44:29.859347-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented relative date formatting (format_relative_date) with calendar year check, and CSV escaping for carriage returns.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"xf-19","depends_on_id":"xf-12","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"}]}
 {"id":"xf-20","title":"Stats Dashboard: Clean & Informative","description":"## Purpose

Make the stats dashboard visually clean and informative. Stats should give users a quick overview of their archive without overwhelming detail.

## Current Problems

1. **Inconsistent emoji usage**:
   Uses emojis for some sections but not items within sections.
   Creates visual inconsistency.

2. **Layout could be cleaner**:
   Current layout mixes single-column and multi-column.
   Alignment is sometimes off.

3. **Sparklines not explained**:
   Activity sparklines are shown but users may not understand them.
   No legend or explanation.

4. **No version info shown**:
   Stats don't show xf version or archive format version.

5. **Large numbers hard to read**:
   '1234567' harder to parse than '1,234,567'.

6. **Missing quick insights**:
   Could show: 'Your most active month was March 2024'
   Or: 'You tweet most on Tuesdays at 2pm'

## Solution

### Clean Layout
Two-column layout with clear sections:
```
ARCHIVE OVERVIEW
───────────────────────────────────────────────────

  Content                        Connections
  ─────────────────              ─────────────────
  Tweets      12,345             Followers   1,234
  Likes        8,234             Following     567
  DMs          2,341             Blocks         12
  Grok           456             Mutes          34

  Timeline: Jan 2015 → Dec 2024 (9 years)
```

### Sparkline Legend
```
Monthly Activity (2024)
▁▂▄▆█▇▅▃▂▁
J F M A M J J A S O N D

Peak: March (1,234 tweets)
```

### Version Info
Show in stats header:
'xf v0.5.0 • Archive: @username • Generated: Jan 2025'

### Quick Insights (--detailed flag)
'Top insights:
  • Your most active month: March 2024 (1,234 tweets)
  • Peak tweeting time: Tuesdays at 2pm
  • Most used hashtag: #rust (234 times)
  • Most mentioned: @github (89 times)'

## Files to Modify

- src/stats_analytics.rs: Main formatting logic
- src/main.rs: cmd_stats() handler
- Cargo.toml: Version constant

## Implementation Notes

Use env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION") to get version at compile time.

Consider terminal width detection for adaptive layout (may be complex).","notes":"Align stats layout with design system (headers/columns) and keep JSON/CSV shapes unchanged. Test plan: unit tests for stats formatting helpers (counts, sparklines, insight text) plus an e2e script that runs xf stats on a fixture archive and logs command, stdout/stderr, exit code, timing, and env.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T09:49:54.508734-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-01-11T10:54:07.419569866-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"xf-20","depends_on_id":"xf-12","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"},{"issue_id":"xf-20","depends_on_id":"xf-13","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"},{"issue_id":"xf-20","depends_on_id":"xf-19","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"}]}
 {"id":"xf-21","title":"Standardize divider widths across codebase","description":"## Task

Audit all uses of '─'.repeat(N) and '═'.repeat(N) in the codebase and standardize them.

## Current State

- stats_analytics.rs: repeat(40), repeat(65)
- main.rs: repeat(60), repeat(65)
- repl.rs: repeat(60)
- doctor output: repeat(60), repeat(65)

## Target State

- Content dividers: 60 characters
- Major section headers: 70 characters
- Use '─' for content, '═' for major headers only

## Implementation

1. grep -n '\.repeat(' src/*.rs to find all occurrences
2. Replace inconsistent widths with standard values
3. Consider creating constants: const DIVIDER_WIDTH: usize = 60;

## Files to Modify

- src/main.rs
- src/repl.rs
- src/stats_analytics.rs
- src/doctor.rs (if any direct output)

## Testing

Visual inspection of: xf stats, xf doctor, xf shell, xf search

## Acceptance Criteria

- All dividers use consistent widths
- No visual regression in any command output","notes":"Standardized divider widths via shared constants (60 content, 70 header) and applied across main/repl output.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T09:50:14.403126-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-01-11T10:11:36.647548103-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T10:03:23.556514-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented --no-color flag (respects NO_COLOR env), standardized dividers to 60/70 chars","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"xf-21","depends_on_id":"xf-13","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"}]}

@@ -0,0 +1,132 @@ +#!/usr/bin/env bash +set -euo pipefail + +ROOT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/../.." && pwd)" +LOG_DIR="$ROOT_DIR/test-output" +mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR" +LOG_FILE="$LOG_DIR/progress_feedback_e2e_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).log" + +XF_BIN="${XF_BIN:-$ROOT_DIR/target/debug/xf}" + +log() { + local msg="$*" + local ts + ts="$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S.%3N')" + echo "[$ts] $msg" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE" +} + +fail() { + log "FAIL: $*" + exit 1 +} + +pass() { + log "PASS: $*" +} + +if [ ! -x "$XF_BIN" ]; then + log "xf binary not found at $XF_BIN; building" + (cd "$ROOT_DIR" && cargo build) +fi + +WORK_DIR="$(mktemp -d)" +trap 'log "Preserving work dir: $WORK_DIR"' EXIT + +ARCHIVE_DIR="$WORK_DIR/archive" +DB_PATH="$WORK_DIR/xf.db" +INDEX_PATH="$WORK_DIR/index" + +mkdir -p "$ARCHIVE_DIR/data" +cat <<'JSON' > "$ARCHIVE_DIR/data/tweets.js" +window.YTD.tweets.part0 = [ + { + "tweet": { + "id_str": "1234567890123456789", + "created_at": "Wed Jan 08 12:00:00 +0000 2025", + "full_text": "Hello world! This is a test tweet about Rust programming.", + "source": "X Web App", + "favorite_count": "42", + "retweet_count": "7", + "lang": "en", + "entities": {"hashtags": [], "user_mentions": [], "urls": []} + } + } +] +JSON +cat <<'JSON' > "$ARCHIVE_DIR/data/manifest.js" +window.YTD.manifest.part0 = { + "userInfo": { + "accountId": "999999999", + "userName": "test_user", + "displayName": "Test User" + }, + "archiveInfo": { + "sizeBytes": "1234", + "generationDate": "2025-01-01T00:00:00Z", + "isPartialArchive": false + } +} +JSON + +LAST_STDOUT="" +LAST_STDERR="" +LAST_STATUS=0 +LAST_DURATION=0 + +run_cmd() { + local name="$1" + shift + local stdout_file="$WORK_DIR/${name}.out" + local stderr_file="$WORK_DIR/${name}.err" + local start end duration_ms + + log "RUN: $name => $*" + start=$(date +%s%N) + if "$@" >"$stdout_file" 2>"$stderr_file"; then + LAST_STATUS=0 + else + LAST_STATUS=$? + fi + end=$(date +%s%N) + duration_ms=$(( (end - start) / 1000000 )) + + LAST_STDOUT="$stdout_file" + LAST_STDERR="$stderr_file" + LAST_DURATION=$duration_ms + + log "EXIT: $LAST_STATUS (duration: ${duration_ms}ms)" + log "STDOUT: $(cat "$stdout_file")" + log "STDERR: $(cat "$stderr_file")" +} + +log "=== progress_feedback_test.sh starting ===" +log "ENV: XF_BIN=$XF_BIN NO_COLOR=1" + +run_cmd "index" env NO_COLOR=1 "$XF_BIN" index "$ARCHIVE_DIR" --db "$DB_PATH" --index "$INDEX_PATH" +if [ $LAST_STATUS -ne 0 ]; then + fail "index command failed" +fi +if ! grep -q "Indexing complete in" "$LAST_STDOUT"; then + fail "index output missing completion timing" +fi +if grep -q $'\x1b' "$LAST_STDOUT"; then + fail "expected no ANSI color codes with NO_COLOR=1 during index" +fi +pass "index timing output present and NO_COLOR honored" + +run_cmd "search" env NO_COLOR=1 "$XF_BIN" search "rust" --db "$DB_PATH" --index "$INDEX_PATH" +if [ $LAST_STATUS -ne 0 ]; then + fail "search command failed" +fi +if ! grep -q "Found" "$LAST_STDOUT"; then + fail "search output missing Found line" +fi +if ! grep -q " in " "$LAST_STDOUT"; then + fail "search output missing timing" +fi +if grep -q $'\x1b' "$LAST_STDOUT"; then + fail "expected no ANSI color codes with NO_COLOR=1 during search" +fi +pass "search timing output present and NO_COLOR honored" + +log "=== progress_feedback_test.sh completed ==="
